user_summary: An empirically-oriented alignment researcher working at the intersection of mechanistic interpretability and character/values training. The three seed papers trace a coherent arc: Constitutional AI as foundational RLHF/CAI methodology, Towards Monosemanticity as the SAE/feature-geometry grounding, and Teaching Claude Why as the current frontier — training interventions that generalize alignment out-of-distribution via document-based updates to base model priors rather than chat demonstrations. Their animating question is what training actually does mechanistically to install values: whether value-like representations emerge and where, how document-fine-tuning shapes priors differently from RLHF, and whether alignment generalizes beyond its evaluation scenarios. Style is firmly Anthropic-empirical: statistical rigor (SEM, power analysis), activation-level evidence for claimed phenomena, and deep skepticism toward alignment claims lacking empirical grounding. Closely embedded in the Teaching Claude Why / Model Spec Midtraining cluster of work at Anthropic and in Neel Nanda's mech interp tradition.
current_question: How character training works in LLMs — what training interventions actually instill values vs. surface behaviors — and how to evaluate model character rigorously (spec compliance evals, red-teaming, statistical methodology).
interests: character training interventions that instill values
activation probing for values and truthfulness
constitutional document effects on model priors
deceptive alignment and hidden objective detection
statistical power analysis for alignment evaluations
SAE evaluation benchmarks and sanity checks
emotion and introspection representations in language models
synthetic training data for alignment generalization

authors.why: Co-authored Model Spec Midtraining (MSM); directly studies how alignment training generalizes OOD; PETRI open-source auditing contributor.

authors.name: Samuel Marks
authors.why: Leads cognitive oversight subteam; probing for deception and hidden objectives; co-authored Teaching Claude Why and MSM; CCS/linear truth-representation work.

authors.why: Primary author of Claude's model spec (Jan 2026 version); leads personality/character alignment; central to all character training methodology at Anthropic.

authors.name: Adam Jermyn
authors.why: Co-led Teaching Claude Why; empirical study of training interventions that reduce agentic misalignment; OOD training environment design.

filter_outs: vague existential risk narratives without empirical grounding
notes: The three seed papers form a tight empirical cluster: CAI (foundational RLHF/constitutional methodology), Monosemanticity (SAE feature-geometry grounding), and Teaching Claude Why (2026, character training that generalizes OOD) — all Anthropic-origin and mechanistically oriented. The followed authors plus freeform context (Neel Nanda, Samuel Marks as close collaborators) confirmed the Anthropic-empirical tradition. For additional authors I mined the full Teaching Claude Why and MSM author lists (Askell, Jermyn, Kutasov, Hubinger, Lindsey, Bowman, Leike) and added Gurnee and Lawrence Chan as the strongest adjacent names in world-representations and scalable-oversight mech interp. Sources were each verified active in 2026 via WebFetch or confirmed-recent search results: alignment.anthropic.com and transformer-circuits.pub are the direct institutional homes; Goodfire and Apollo provide independent empirical mech interp and scheming-detection coverage; FAR AI covers adversarial robustness evals; the ICML 2026 Mech Interp Workshop is the field's current aggregation point. Alignment Forum was excluded per instructions. Apollo's work skews toward scheming/deception but overlaps meaningfully with spec compliance and hidden-objective detection.
